prompt:
So I keep a couple hundred markdown notes in one flat folder, Obsidian-style, and I want to publish them as plain HTML I can rsync anywhere. I started writing this three times and always got lost in the backlink bookkeeping, so I'm handing it over. Deliverable is a compiler, `wikic.py`, with two subcommands: python3 wikic.py build python3 wikic.py check Pages: every .md file in srcdir (flat, no recursion needed; ignore non-.md files). First line must be `# Title` -- that's the page title; a page without it should make the build fail with exit 1 and the offending filename on stderr. After that the format is dead simple: blank-line-separated paragraphs, and lines inside one paragraph get joined with single spaces into one

. All text HTML-escaped (I write about code, `&` and `<` show up). Links are the point of the whole thing. `[[Some Page]]` or `[[Some Page|display text]]` anywhere in a paragraph. A page's slug is its filename stem; to resolve a link, slugify the target (lowercase, every run of non-alphanumeric characters becomes a single hyphen, trim hyphens at the ends) and match it against page slugs -- so [[Getting Started]] finds getting-started.md. Resolved links become display text (or the target as written); unresolved ones become ... and do NOT fail the build. Each page compiles to .html: the

title, the paragraphs, and then -- if any OTHER page links to it -- a

Backlinks

section listing links to those pages, sorted by title, each linking page listed once no matter how many times it linked. A page linking to itself doesn't count. Plus an index.html listing every page as a link, sorted by title. Builds must be byte-for-byte deterministic and create outdir if needed. Nonexistent srcdir = usage error, exit 2. check is my lint: print `broken: -> ` for every unresolvable wikilink, sorted by source then target, then `orphan: ` for every page no other page links to, sorted -- except the home page (slug `home`), which is allowed to be unlinked since it's the landing page. Anything printed -> exit 1; a clean wiki prints just `ok` and exits 0. Don't jam it all into wikic.py -- I'll want to poke at the pieces (parsing / link resolution / rendering / the lint) separately. The acceptance contract is test_wikic.py; `python3 test_wikic.py` must pass without you touching it.
